title: practical english usage - Part 21

Here is a list of some common verbs which  are not often used in progressive 2 common       non-progressive           verbs Modal  verbs (e.g. can, must) have no progressive forms, ►    68.1.

the senses (e.g. smell, taste).

refer to mental states (e.g. know, think, believe); some others refer to the use of Many of these non-progressive verbs refer to states rather than actions. Some not I see what you mean. (     I'm seeing what you mean.) I'm seeing the doctor at ten o'clock.

meanings (see below). Compare: Some other verbs are not used in progressive forms when they have certain not I called her because I needed to talk. (     ... because I was needing to talk.) not I like this music. (     I'm  liking this musie.) Some verbs are not often used in progressive forms.

1  verbs     not   often     used    in  progressive         ('continuous')       forms 4 non-progressive           verbs    ___________ progressive, etc.

For more details of the use of progressives, see the individual entries on the present progressive, past For more about this kind of distancing, ►    311.

Will you be going away at the weekend?

I was wondering if you had two single rooms.

I'm afraid we must be going.

I'm looking forward to seeing you again.

What time are you planning to arrive?

I'm hoping you can lend me £10. (less definite than I hope...) temporary and incomplete.) (They sound less definite than simple forms, because they suggest something Progressives can make requests, questions and statements less direct.

3 distancing:       I was     wondering            ...

grammar •  4  non-progressive verbs I've been meaning to tell you about Andrew. He.. .

I've been wanting to go to Australia for years.

Will you be needing the car this afternoon?

Need, want and mean can have future or present perfect progressive uses.

I'm liking it here more and more as time goes by.

As I get older, I remember / I'm remembering less and less.

The water tastes / is tasting better today.

These days, more and more people prefer / are preferring to retire early.

emphasise the idea of change or development.

Occasionally ‘ non-progressive’  verbs are used in progressive forms in order to weighing 68 kilos ...- weigh here =  have weight.) not   was I weighed 68 kilos three months ago - and look at me now! (     I - The scales broke when I was weighing myself this morning.
